quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, is named after a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in 1592. He claimed to have found a passage between the Pacific and Atlantic oceans—though the strait he found doesn't quite go that far, his name lives on in these beautiful waters!

short_read.title: How Hummingbirds Fly in the Rain
short_read.teaser: These tiny aerial acrobats have amazing adaptations that let them fly even in heavy downpours. Scientists have discovered their secret involves special feather structures and mid-air shaking moves.

medium_read.title: The Hidden Life of Garden Soil
medium_read.teaser: Beneath every garden lies an invisible world teeming with billions of creatures working together. This fascinating look at soil reveals why a handful of healthy earth contains more living organisms than there are people on the planet.

long_read.title: The Secret Resistance: Hungary's Forgotten Heroes of World War II
long_read.teaser: While much of Hungary's wartime story is well known, countless everyday Hungarians risked everything to save their neighbors. This moving account honors the quiet courage of ordinary people who made extraordinary choices.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer in Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: July is a wonderful time to spot black-tailed deer with their fawns in Hurricane Ridge. The young fawns, born in late spring, are now old enough to follow their mothers through the meadows, and you might see them grazing on the wildflowers that blanket the hillsides this time of year.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while hovering.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right, Barbara! That tiny heart works incredibly hard to power all that hovering!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute—imagine that!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: A hummingbird's heart actually beats about 1,200 times per minute! That's 20 times every single second to keep those wings going.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To put this in perspective, a human heart beats about 60-100 times per minute at rest. The hummingbird's racing heartbeat helps deliver oxygen fast enough to power wings that beat 50-80 times per second!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Ildiko! Pillangó is indeed butterfly—such a lovely, musical word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound beautiful? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The word 'pillangó' comes from an old Hungarian root meaning 'to flutter.' Hungary is home to over 180 species of butterflies, including the beautiful swallowtails and peacock butterflies.

puzzle_3.question: What unique feature helps sea otters, common in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, stay warm in cold water?
options: A layer of blubber like whales
The densest fur of any animal
Heated blood vessels in their paws
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Sea otters have up to one million hairs per square inch—that's the densest fur in the animal kingdom, keeping them cozy in our cold Pacific waters!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Sea otters actually have the densest fur of any animal—up to a million hairs per square inch! Unlike whales and seals, they don't have blubber, so that luxurious fur coat is their key to staying warm.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Sea otters spend hours each day grooming their fur to maintain its insulating properties. They blow air into their fur to create a waterproof layer, which is why you'll often see them looking so fluffy when floating on their backs!

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
